steps.title: Find the decisive clue
steps.content: The claim centers on Newland's “tendency to follow the social conventions dictated by other men.” The strongest signal is text showing collective masculine influence forcing Newland to adopt the group's standards.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice A directly shows Newland adopting the group's standards: “the habit of masculine solidarity made him accept their doctrine” reveals how the grouped men exert pressure that Newland submits to on moral issues. The other three choices depict scenery, position Newland as self-importantly superior to the group, or discuss architectural details. That matches choice A — A is correct.
